original_hypothesis.title: TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable Owner Can Arbitrarily Manipulate L2 Gas Pricing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The owner of TaikoL2EIP1559Configurable can call setConfigAndExcess to set gasExcess to type(uint64).max, causing the base fee to spike to astronomical levels (making all L2 transactions prohibitively expensive). Alternatively, setting gasExcess=0 permanently disables dynamic fees (basefee=1 wei). This manipulation affects all L2 users immediately with no timelock or governance delay.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: setConfigAndExcess only validates gasTargetPerL1Block and basefeeAdjustmentQuotient are non-zero, but places no bounds on gasExcess. Combined with the cross-chain ownership model, this creates a powerful lever that could be exploited by a compromised L1 owner.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No bounds check on _newGasExcess; allows setting to 0 (disable) or type(uint64).max (spike)

preconditions: Owner calls setConfigAndExcess with extreme gasExcess value

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: gasExcess=type(uint64).max → extreme basefee; gasExcess=0 → basefee=1 permanently
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No reasonable bounds check, no timelock, no rate limiting

analysis_questions: What is the basefee when gasExcess=type(uint64).max with default config?
Is the owner behind a timelock governance structure?
Can users detect and exit before the config change takes effect?
validation_requirements: Calculate basefee for extreme gasExcess values
Verify setConfigAndExcess has no delay mechanism
Check if anchor() enforces any basefee sanity bounds

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised owner calls setConfigAndExcess(config, type(uint64).max). The next anchor transaction calculates an extremely high basefee. All pending L2 transactions become unaffordable. Alternatively, setting gasExcess=0 makes basefee=1 wei permanently, enabling network spam.
prerequisites: Owner key compromise or malicious governance

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: L2 network DoS (extremely high fees) or spam vulnerability (1 wei fees)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner can restore reasonable values if not locked out

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for extreme gasExcess values

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Takes effect on next anchor transaction

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
